endpoints.description: Endpoint that serves Prometheus metrics.
Return process liveness for the service runtime.
Return dependency-aware readiness for the service runtime.
Returns portfolios with optional filtering by portfolio ID, CIF, and booking center. Used by UI/lotus-gateway for portfolio discovery and navigation.

endpoints.summary: Get a Single Portfolio by ID
endpoints.description: Returns the canonical portfolio record for a single portfolio identifier.

endpoints.summary: Get Position History for a Security
endpoints.description: Returns epoch-aware position history for a portfolio-security key across a date range. Used for drill-down views and lineage-aware troubleshooting.

endpoints.summary: Get Latest Positions for a Portfolio
endpoints.description: Returns latest current-epoch positions for a portfolio. Used by holdings screens and downstream review/analytics flows.

endpoints.summary: Get canonical cash accounts for a portfolio
endpoints.description: Returns source-owned canonical cash-account master records for one portfolio. Use this endpoint for canonical account identity and lifecycle metadata; use reporting cash-balances when balances and translated totals are required.

endpoints.summary: Get BUY Lot State for a Position
endpoints.description: Returns durable BUY lot records for a portfolio-security key, including linkage and policy metadata used for lifecycle traceability.
Returns accrued-income offset state initialized by BUY events for a portfolio-security key.
Returns security-side BUY linkage fields and linked cashflow details for reconciliation.

endpoints.summary: Get SELL Disposal State for a Position
endpoints.description: Returns SELL disposal records for a portfolio-security key, including disposed quantity, disposed cost basis, realized P&L, and policy/linkage metadata for audit and reconciliation.
Returns security-side SELL linkage fields and linked settlement cashflow details for deterministic reconciliation.
Returns transactions for a portfolio with date-window filters, optional instrument filtering, pagination, and sorting. Designed for transaction ledgers, audit timelines, and investigative support.
Returns portfolio-level daily net cashflow projection for operational liquidity planning. Supports booked-only mode and projected mode for future-dated trades.
Returns reference instrument records with optional filtering by security and product type. Used by lookup selectors and enrichment workflows.

endpoints.summary: Get Market Prices for a Security
endpoints.description: Returns market price series for a security with optional date-range filtering. Used by valuation checks and market data diagnostics.

endpoints.summary: Get FX Rates for a Currency Pair
endpoints.description: Returns FX rates for a currency pair over an optional date range. Used by valuation/performance conversion diagnostics and reconciliation.
Returns portfolio selector options for lotus-gateway/UI portfolio selection workflows.
Returns instrument selector options for lotus-gateway/UI trade and intake workflows.
Returns distinct currency selector options derived from portfolio base currencies and instrument currencies.

Returns assets-under-management views for a single portfolio, an explicit portfolio list, or a business unit (booking center). Designed for PB/WM dashboards and reporting packs using latest-snapshot, as-of-date semantics.

Returns allocation views grouped by Lotus-supported classification dimensions such as asset class, currency, sector, country, product type, rating, and issuer hierarchy. Optimized for interactive reporting over resolved portfolio scopes.

Returns portfolio cash accounts, native balances, and translated totals in portfolio currency and reporting currency for UI and reporting workflows. For large-scale export workflows, prefer a dedicated async export contract.

Returns a true historical as-of portfolio summary for one portfolio, including market value totals, cash versus invested split, and snapshot coverage metadata. Use this contract when UI or reporting consumers need a restated summary in portfolio currency and reporting currency without rebuilding holdings totals client-side.

Returns a true historical as-of holdings snapshot for one portfolio with reporting-currency restatement and portfolio-workspace classifications. Use this contract for UI holdings views and reporting extracts that need region-aware, restated holdings rows.

Returns income totals for the requested reporting window and year-to-date, with values in portfolio currency and reporting currency. Income is grouped by canonical Lotus income transaction types such as dividend, interest, and cash-in-lieu.

Returns portfolio-level flow buckets for the requested reporting window and year-to-date. The summary is intentionally scoped to portfolio flows: inflows, outflows, fees, and taxes, with values translated to portfolio currency and reporting currency.
Endpoint that serves Prometheus metrics.
Return process liveness for the service runtime.
Return dependency-aware readiness for the service runtime.

What: Accept canonical portfolio master records.
How: Validate portfolio schema, enforce idempotency/mode checks, and publish asynchronously for persistence.
When: Use when onboarding or updating portfolio metadata from upstream systems.

What: Accept one canonical transaction record for ledger ingestion.
How: Validate contract, enforce idempotency/mode controls, then publish asynchronously to Kafka.
When: Use for low-volume operational corrections or single-record onboarding.

What: Accept a batch of canonical transaction records.
How: Persist ingestion job metadata, validate payload, and publish all valid records asynchronously.
When: Use for standard API-driven batch ingestion workflows.

What: Accept canonical instrument/security reference records.
How: Validate schema, enforce ingestion mode/idempotency controls, and publish to asynchronous persistence pipeline.
When: Use for security master onboarding and reference data corrections.

What: Accept canonical market price observations for securities.
How: Validate payload, enforce ingestion guardrails, and publish asynchronous events for valuation processing.
When: Use for daily close pricing loads or intraday approved market data updates.

What: Accept canonical foreign-exchange rate observations.
How: Validate FX rate contract, enforce ingestion controls, and publish asynchronous events for downstream valuation.
When: Use for scheduled FX reference updates and approved manual corrections.

What: Accept canonical business calendar dates used by valuation and processing lifecycles.
How: Validate date records, apply ingestion controls, and publish asynchronous persistence events.
When: Use for calendar setup, holiday updates, and date-correction operations.

What: Accept transaction identifiers that require deterministic historical recalculation.
How: Validate request, persist ingestion job metadata, and publish reprocessing command events.
When: Use for operational correction workflows after retroactive data changes.

What: Accept a mixed onboarding bundle containing portfolio, instrument, transaction, market-price, FX-rate, and business-date records.
How: Validate adapter payload and fan out records into existing canonical ingestion topics.
When: Use for adapter-mode onboarding (UI/manual/file workflows), not primary upstream integration.

endpoints.summary: Preview and validate bulk upload data
endpoints.description:
What: Validate CSV/XLSX ingestion payloads without publishing records.
How: Parse file rows, apply entity-specific schema checks, and return row-level validation feedback.
When: Use before commit to catch data-quality issues in bulk adapter uploads.

What: Commit CSV/XLSX data into canonical ingestion topics.
How: Validate rows, enforce mode controls, and publish valid records (optionally partial when allowPartial=true).
When: Use after preview passes for adapter-mode bulk ingestion.

What: Accept effective-dated benchmark assignment records for portfolios.
How: Validate canonical contract, enforce ingestion controls, and upsert durable records.
When: Use for benchmark onboarding, assignment updates, and restatement correction cycles.

What: Accept benchmark master definition records.
How: Validate canonical contract and upsert effective-dated benchmark metadata.
When: Use for benchmark master onboarding and lifecycle updates.

What: Accept benchmark composition time-series records.
How: Validate composition weights and upsert effective-dated component memberships.
When: Use for benchmark rebalance and composition history maintenance.

What: Accept index master definition records.
How: Validate canonical contract and upsert effective-dated index metadata.
When: Use for index onboarding and attribution metadata lifecycle updates.

What: Accept raw index price series records.
How: Validate canonical contract and upsert time-series observations.
When: Use for daily market close processing and historical backfills.

What: Accept raw vendor-provided index return series records.
How: Validate return conventions and upsert deterministic time-series rows.
When: Use when upstream publishes return series directly.

What: Accept raw vendor-provided benchmark return series records.
How: Validate return conventions and upsert deterministic time-series rows.
When: Use when benchmark return series is provided by upstream vendor.

What: Accept risk-free reference series records.
How: Validate convention fields and upsert deterministic time-series rows.
When: Use for benchmark-relative analytics and risk-adjusted metric inputs.

What: Accept classification taxonomy records used for attribution alignment.
How: Validate canonical dimensions and upsert effective-dated taxonomy entries.
When: Use when platform taxonomy labels are introduced or updated.

What: Accept canonical cash-account master records.
How: Validate PB/WM cash-account identity and upsert durable portfolio/account linkage metadata.
When: Use during onboarding and cash-account lifecycle maintenance.

What: Accept effective-dated look-through composition rows for funds and structured products.
How: Validate component weights and upsert durable decomposition records.
When: Use when downstream allocation consumers need source-owned look-through views.
